back_translation: Early the next morning Joshua got up and they set out from Shittim. They came as far as the Jordan, together with all the descendants of Israel, and they camped overnight there before they crossed over.
When three days had ended, the officers went throughout the camp

and commanded the people, saying, "When you-pl see the ark of the covenant of the Lord your-pl God, and the Levitical priests carrying it, then you-pl shall move from your-pl place and follow after it.

But keep a distance between you-pl and it of about two thousand cubits in measure. Do not come near it, so that you-pl may know the way which you-pl shall walk on, for you-pl have not previously traveled this way."
Then Joshua said to the people, "Consecrate yourselves, for tomorrow the Lord will do wonders among you-pl."
Joshua said to the priests, "Lift up the ark of the covenant and cross over before the people." So they lifted up the ark of the covenant and walked ahead of the people.
The Lord said to Joshua, "On this day I will begin to exalt you in the eyes of all Israel, so that they may know: just as I was with Moses, I will be with you.
You-sg shall command the priests carrying the ark of the covenant, saying, 'When you-pl come to the edge of the waters of the Jordan, you-pl shall stand still in the Jordan.'"
Joshua said to the descendants of Israel, "Come here and listen to the words of the Lord your-pl God."

Joshua continued, "By this you-pl will know that the living God is in your-pl midst, and that He will surely drive out before you-pl the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Hivites, the Perizzites, the Girgashites, the Amorites, and the Jebusites.
Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth is about to cross before you-pl into the Jordan.
Now therefore choose for yourselves twelve men from the tribes of Israel, one man from each tribe.

And it shall be that when the soles of the feet of the priests carrying the ark of the Lord, the Lord of all the earth, touch the waters of the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan will be cut off — the water flowing down from above — and will stand up as one heap."
So when the people set out from their tents to cross the Jordan, the priests carrying the ark of the covenant were ahead of the people.
When those carrying the ark came to the Jordan, and the feet of the priests carrying the ark were dipped in the edge of the water (the Jordan overflows all its banks throughout the harvest season)

the flowing water from above stood still and rose up as one heap, very far away at the city of Adam, the city beside Zarethan; while the water flowing down toward the Sea of the Arabah, namely the Salt Sea, was completely cut off. So the people crossed over opposite Jericho.

The priests carrying the ark of the covenant of the Lord stood firm on dry ground in the midst of the Jordan, while all Israel crossed over on the dry ground, until the whole nation had finished crossing the Jordan.
